title: Rootless buildah-run name resolution fails when using local caching DNS server on host
body:
**Description**

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. Host network setup with local DNS server. Easiest way is to have networking setup using NetworkManager configured with `dns=dnsmasq`. Check that `/etc/resolv.conf` on host has `nameserver 127.0.0.1`
```
$  cat /etc/NetworkManager/conf.d/20-dns.conf
[main]
dns=dnsmasq

$ cat /etc/resolv.conf
# Generated by NetworkManager
search redhat.com
nameserver 127.0.0.1
```
2. Run nslookup from a rootless buildah container.
```
$ buildah from alpine:latest
$ buildah run -t alpine-working-container nslookup google.com
nslookup: can't resolve '(null)': Name does not resolve

nslookup: can't resolve 'google.com': Try again
error while running runtime: exit status 1
ERRO[0005] exit status 1
```


**Describe the results you received:**
nslookup fails as the address cannot be resolved. 

buildah copies the host resolv.conf into the container. But the container cannot reach the host dnsmasq daemon as configured in resolv.conf. This causes name resolution failures, preventing most network operations from working from within the container.

**Describe the results you expected:**

nslookup should succeed.

**Additional information**
Setting nameserver to 8.8.8.8 in `resolv.conf` in the container allows it work correctly.
```
$ buildah run -t alpine-working-container ash
/ # nslookup google.com
nslookup: can't resolve '(null)': Name does not resolve

nslookup: can't resolve 'google.com': Try again
/ # echo nameserver 8.8.8.8 > /etc/resolv.conf
/ # nslookup google.com
nslookup: can't resolve '(null)': Name does not resolve

Name:      google.com
Address 1: 172.217.163.142 maa05s04-in-f14.1e100.net
Address 2: 2404:6800:4007:80f::200e maa05s05-in-x0e.1e100.net
/ #
```

buildah <v1.5 worked okay. The change in v1.5 to use slirp4netns to setup network for rootless containers causes the current issue. Prior to v1.5 I believe that rootless buildah containers used the host network directly.

Rootless podman execution works as podman sets up `/etc/resolv.conf` in the container.
```
$  podman run --rm -t -i alpine:latest ash
/ # nslookup google.com
nslookup: can't resolve '(null)': Name does not resolve

Name:      google.com
Address 1: 172.217.163.110 maa05s03-in-f14.1e100.net
Address 2: 2404:6800:4007:808::200e maa05s10-in-x0e.1e100.net
/ # cat /etc/resolv.conf 
search redhat.com
nameserver 8.8.8.8
nameserver 8.8.4.4
nameserver 2001:4860:4860::8888
nameserver 2001:4860:4860::8844
/ #
```

**Output of `rpm -q buildah` or `apt list buildah`:**

```
$ pacman -Ql buildah
buildah /usr/
buildah /usr/bin/
buildah /usr/bin/buildah
buildah /usr/share/
buildah /usr/share/licenses/
buildah /usr/share/licenses/buildah/
buildah /usr/share/licenses/buildah/LICENSE
buildah /usr/share/man/
buildah /usr/share/man/man1/
buildah /usr/share/man/man1/buildah-add.1.gz
buildah /usr/share/man/man1/buildah-bud.1.gz
buildah /usr/share/man/man1/buildah-commit.1.gz
buildah /usr/share/man/man1/buildah-config.1.gz
buildah /usr/share/man/man1/buildah-containers.1.gz
buildah /usr/share/man/man1/buildah-copy.1.gz
buildah /usr/share/man/man1/buildah-from.1.gz
buildah /usr/share/man/man1/buildah-images.1.gz
buildah /usr/share/man/man1/buildah-inspect.1.gz
buildah /usr/share/man/man1/buildah-mount.1.gz
buildah /usr/share/man/man1/buildah-pull.1.gz
buildah /usr/share/man/man1/buildah-push.1.gz
buildah /usr/share/man/man1/buildah-rename.1.gz
buildah /usr/share/man/man1/buildah-rm.1.gz
buildah /usr/share/man/man1/buildah-rmi.1.gz
buildah /usr/share/man/man1/buildah-run.1.gz
buildah /usr/share/man/man1/buildah-tag.1.gz
buildah /usr/share/man/man1/buildah-umount.1.gz
buildah /usr/share/man/man1/buildah-unshare.1.gz
buildah /usr/share/man/man1/buildah-version.1.gz
buildah /usr/share/man/man1/buildah.1.gz
```

**Output of `buildah version`:**

```
$  buildah version
Version:         1.5
Go Version:      go1.11.2
Image Spec:      1.0.0
Runtime Spec:    1.0.0
CNI Spec:        0.4.0
libcni Version:  v0.7.0-alpha1
Git Commit:      e94b4f98
Built:           Wed Nov 28 15:40:56 2018
OS/Arch:         linux/amd64
```

**Output of `cat /etc/*release`:**

```
$ cat /etc/*release
Arch Linux release
LSB_VERSION=1.4
DISTRIB_ID=Arch
DISTRIB_RELEASE=rolling
DISTRIB_DESCRIPTION="Arch Linux"
NAME="Arch Linux"
PRETTY_NAME="Arch Linux"
ID=arch
ID_LIKE=archlinux
ANSI_COLOR="0;36"
HOME_URL="https://www.archlinux.org/"
SUPPORT_URL="https://bbs.archlinux.org/"
BUG_REPORT_URL="https://bugs.archlinux.org/"
```

**Output of `uname -a`:**

```
$  uname -a
Linux arch 4.19.4-arch1-1-ARCH #1 SMP PREEMPT Fri Nov 23 09:06:58 UTC 2018 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

comments.body:
If you run with --net=host, does it work correctly?
@giuseppe PTAL

`--net=host` works. 
```
$ buildah run alpine-working-container nslookup google.com
nslookup: can't resolve '(null)': Name does not resolve

nslookup: can't resolve 'google.com': Try again
error while running runtime: exit status 1
ERRO[0006] exit status 1                                
$ buildah run --net=host alpine-working-container nslookup google.com 
nslookup: can't resolve '(null)': Name does not resolve

Name:      google.com
Address 1: 216.58.200.142 maa05s10-in-f14.1e100.net
Address 2: 2404:6800:4007:808::200e maa05s10-in-x0e.1e100.net
$
```

`/etc/resolv.conf` should be a bind mount from the host as part of: https://github.com/containers/buildah/commit/3cebe4f2c4de252e707ccaffbc93e30201b05ea5.

Following the same steps:

```
$ buildah from alpine
alpine-working-container
$ buildah run -t alpine-working-container ash
/ # nslookup google.com
nslookup: can't resolve '(null)': Name does not resolve

Name:      google.com
Address 1: 216.58.215.110 waw02s17-in-f14.1e100.net
Address 2: 2a00:1450:401b:801::200e waw02s06-in-x0e.1e100.net
/ # wget google.com
Connecting to google.com (216.58.215.110:80)
Connecting to www.google.com (172.217.22.4:80)
index.html           100% |*********************************************************************************************************************************************| 11695   0:00:00 ETA
/ # ifconfig
lo        Link encap:Local Loopback  
          inet addr:127.0.0.1  Mask:255.0.0.0
          inet6 addr: ::1/128 Scope:Host
          UP LOOPBACK RUNNING  MTU:65536  Metric:1
          RX packets:0 errors:0 dropped:0 overruns:0 frame:0
          TX packets:0 errors:0 dropped:0 overruns:0 carrier:0
          collisions:0 txqueuelen:1000 
          RX bytes:0 (0.0 B)  TX bytes:0 (0.0 B)

tap0      Link encap:Ethernet  HWaddr F6:05:5D:D8:48:DA  
          inet addr:10.0.2.100  Bcast:10.0.2.255  Mask:255.255.255.0
          inet6 addr: fe80::f405:5dff:fed8:48da/64 Scope:Link
          UP BROADCAST RUNNING  MTU:1500  Metric:1
          RX packets:44 errors:0 dropped:0 overruns:0 frame:0
          TX packets:71 errors:0 dropped:0 overruns:0 carrier:0
          collisions:0 txqueuelen:1000 
          RX bytes:16660 (16.2 KiB)  TX bytes:6199 (6.0 KiB)
```

How does your `ifconfig` inside the container look like?

Also, can you double check if the slirp4netns is running while you use the container?

> `/etc/resolv.conf` should be a bind mount from the host as part of: [3cebe4f](https://github.com/containers/buildah/commit/3cebe4f2c4de252e707ccaffbc93e30201b05ea5)

Yes, it is the same in my case as well. This is causing the issue.

> How does your `ifconfig` inside the container look like?

```
$ buildah run alpine-working-container ash
/ # ifconfig
lo        Link encap:Local Loopback  
          inet addr:127.0.0.1  Mask:255.0.0.0
          inet6 addr: ::1/128 Scope:Host
          UP LOOPBACK RUNNING  MTU:65536  Metric:1
          RX packets:0 errors:0 dropped:0 overruns:0 frame:0
          TX packets:0 errors:0 dropped:0 overruns:0 carrier:0
          collisions:0 txqueuelen:1000 
          RX bytes:0 (0.0 B)  TX bytes:0 (0.0 B)

tap0      Link encap:Ethernet  HWaddr FA:21:D6:E4:B7:62  
          inet addr:10.0.2.100  Bcast:10.0.2.255  Mask:255.255.255.0
          inet6 addr: fe80::f821:d6ff:fee4:b762/64 Scope:Link
          UP BROADCAST RUNNING  MTU:1500  Metric:1
          RX packets:0 errors:0 dropped:0 overruns:0 frame:0
          TX packets:6 errors:0 dropped:0 overruns:0 carrier:0
          collisions:0 txqueuelen:1000 
          RX bytes:0 (0.0 B)  TX bytes:516 (516.0 B)
```



> Also, can you double check if the slirp4netns is running while you use the container?

slirp4netns is running when using the containers.
```
$  pstree -a -p 1316
buildah,1316 run alpine-working-container ash
  ├─exe,1327 run alpine-working-container ash
  │   ├─exe,1392
```

I was curious and checked what libpod does. libpod filters out localhost nameserver entries when using netns. And if no more nameservers are present after filtering out, it sets google DNS servers. Buildah should be doing the same. https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/pkg/resolvconf/resolvconf.go#L105

I can confirm the issue, and with `bud` net=host does not solve the issue:

This currently breaks any rootless builds on e.g. Fedora with dnssec-trigger-script installed:

```
$ cat Dockerfile.test 
FROM registry.centos.org/centos/centos:7

SHELL ["/bin/sh", "-c"]

RUN curl -I google.com
$ buildah bud --net=host -f Dockerfile.test foo
STEP 1: FROM registry.centos.org/centos/centos:7
STEP 2: SHELL ["/bin/sh", "-c"]
STEP 3: RUN curl -I google.com
curl: (6) Could not resolve host: google.com; Unknown error
error building at step {Env:[PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin] Command:run Args:[curl -I google.com] Flags:[] Attrs:map[] Message:RUN curl -I google.com Original:RUN curl -I google.com}: error while running runtime: exit status 6
ERRO[0000] exit status 1       
```

Workaround:

```
$ cat Dockerfile.test2
FROM registry.centos.org/centos/centos:7

SHELL ["/bin/sh", "-c"]

# Install required packages
RUN cat /etc/resolv.conf && echo "nameserver 8.8.8.8" > /etc/resolv.conf > /etc/resolv.conf && curl -I google.com
$ buildah bud -f Dockerfile.test2 foo
STEP 1: FROM registry.centos.org/centos/centos:7
STEP 2: SHELL ["/bin/sh", "-c"]
STEP 3: RUN cat /etc/resolv.conf && echo "nameserver 8.8.8.8" > /etc/resolv.conf > /etc/resolv.conf && curl -I google.com
# Generated by dnssec-trigger-script
nameserver 127.0.0.1
HTTP/1.1 301 Moved Permanently
Location: http://www.google.com/
Content-Type: text/html; charset=UTF-8
Date: Mon, 17 Dec 2018 09:14:52 GMT
Expires: Wed, 16 Jan 2019 09:14:52 GMT
Cache-Control: public, max-age=2592000
Server: gws
Content-Length: 219
X-XSS-Protection: 1; mode=block
X-Frame-Options: SAMEORIGIN

ERRO[0000] HOSTNAME is not supported for OCI image format, hostname deb198d77dbc will be ignored. Must use `docker` format 
ERRO[0000] SHELL is not supported for OCI image format, [/bin/sh -c] will be ignored. Must use `docker` format 
STEP 4: COMMIT containers-storage:[overlay@/home/duritong/.local/share/containers/storage+/run/user/1000:overlay.mount_program=/usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs]@4a05657dfb6c2ac8a11debfcf19bd788c7cad93aa7725b15fa9c9f40b0a87de3
Getting image source signatures
Skipping fetch of repeat blob sha256:b05580fca2f9aabb2d8fa975b29146c9147c8418e559f197c54a4fac04babb95
Copying blob sha256:055730e71fd99067d406ef58eaa9a358358bd5708774261b5b17bb247975bd65
 132 B / 132 B [============================================================] 0s
Copying config sha256:b9a1e6a074d2c621c5a29a0dc0c0ae0f99b903ab1b743b52acd430b6bd713e69
 1.17 KiB / 1.17 KiB [======================================================] 0s
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
--> 4a05657dfb6c2ac8a11debfcf19bd788c7cad93aa7725b15fa9c9f40b0a87de3
```

> I was curious and checked what libpod does. libpod filters out localhost nameserver entries when using netns. And if no more nameservers are present after filtering out, it sets google DNS servers. Buildah should be doing the same. https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/pkg/resolvconf/resolvconf.go#L105

To bring the `--dns` option from podman to `buildah run`  and `buildah bud` may help too.

> I can confirm the issue, and with `bud` net=host does not solve the issue:

Same here, `buildah bud` should probably do the same than `buildah run`

@duritong A better workaround for me is to use `--volume $HOME/myresolv.conf:/etc/resolv.conf:ro` in order to avoid modifying all RUN lines
@QiWang19 Could you look into adding this feature.
